title: Label 309 sealed-PoE encryption envelope
description: The `enc` block of an item: a CLOSED CBOR map describing a sealed-PoE envelope whose ciphertext lives at the item's content-addressed URI (ar:// / ipfs://). `scheme` MUST be the unsigned integer 1 — modelled here as an unconstrained value (the structural superset), with the literal-1 requirement enforced by the domain pass (UNSUPPORTED_ENVELOPE_SCHEME). `aead` is the content cipher identifier (open). `nonce` is the AEAD nonce (hex byte string; its exact length is the per-AEAD NONCE_LENGTH_MISMATCH domain check — e.g. 24 bytes for xchacha20-poly1305). `kem`, `slots`, `slots_mac`, and `passphrase` are all OPTIONAL at this structural layer. STRUCTURAL only — the following cross-field invariants are NOT expressible here and are domain-pass checks emitting typed codes: slots XOR passphrase (mutually exclusive); slots requires both kem and slots_mac; slots non-empty; the KEM-driven per-slot shape; the argon2id floors; content-hash binding. The map is closed: unknown keys are rejected.

scheme.$comment: MUST equal unsigned integer 1; modelled as any value structurally (mirrors the permissive Zod `z.unknown()`), value enforced by the domain pass (UNSUPPORTED_ENVELOPE_SCHEME).

nonce.$comment: AEAD nonce, hex byte string; exact length per-AEAD (NONCE_LENGTH_MISMATCH) is a domain-pass check, not asserted structurally.

slots_mac.$comment: 32-byte MAC over the slot set; wrong length emits ENC_SLOTS_MAC_INVALID_LENGTH.
